snippet: Early intervention aims to prevent or minimise the impact of a new work-related injury or illness, and the deterioration of any existing injuries or illnesses. Early intervention recognises the importance of early treatment and support in the workplace to facilitate return to work and return to health outcomes. Benefits of early intervention include that it: School leadership teams can create a safe and supportive environment that protects workers by: available for school leaders and business managers through the Victorian Academy of Teaching and Leadership) promoting the health, safety and wellbeing supports available to all workers, including the Employee Wellbeing Support Services and other expert supports actively supporting a strong reporting culture by encouraging school staff to report all hazards, risks, near misses, incidents and injuries to the principal or their delegate, using eduSafe Plus, and analysing these reports regularly if an injury does arise, taking quick action to contact the worker to discuss supports available to them. Guidance Overview of the Workers' Compensation – Workplace Injury gui... Lodging a claim Includes information on when a worker can lodge a claim, act... Includes information on the benefits of early intervention to prevent or minimise the impact of a new work-related injury or illness Reviewed 20 January 2025
